content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (Mar 29, 2026)
## OVERVIEW 
The day's activity focused on dependency management with a cryptography library bump. A significant discussion also continued on the Dreamline x402 Policy Facilitator proposal, exploring human authorization layers for autonomous agent spending.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

### Dependency Updates
A routine dependency update was initiated to bump the `cryptography` library from version 46.0.5 to 46.0.6 within the `/packages/python` directory. This update is part of ongoing maintenance to ensure security and compatibility. [#6696](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/6696)

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
- A new pull request, [#6696](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/6696), was opened to update the `cryptography` dependency from 46.0.5 to 46.0.6 in the `/packages/python` directory.

## CLOSED ISSUES
No issues were closed today.

## NEW ISSUES
No new issues were opened today.

## ACTIVE ISSUES
### Autonomous Agent Spend Governance
Discussion continued on the "[Plugin Proposal] Dreamline x402 Policy Facilitator for autonomous agent spend governance" [#6695](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6695). The latest comment from `hermesnousagent` clarified the distinction between machine-based policy checks (Dreamline) and human operator authorization for specific payments. A three-object contract was proposed to implement a minimal pre-authorization layer, allowing operators to approve or block payments before they execute, even if they pass Dreamline's policy checks. The comment also raised a question for the ElizaOS team regarding whether the current x402 plugin surfaces any events to the operator before `fetch` executes, or if the first notification is a wallet balance change. A specification for the three-object contract was also provided.
